messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following sports club membership privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"The club collects your name, date of birth, contact details, emergency contacts, and medical conditions relevant to participation. Medical data is collected with your explicit consent to ensure safe participation. Membership data is retained for the duration of membership plus 3 years. Emergency contacts are only used in genuine emergencies. You may access or correct your data by contacting the club secretary. Membership fees are processed by GoCardless under a data processing agreement."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Membership implies contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) but this is not explicitly stated. Explicit consent is stated for medical data.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Medical conditions correctly identified as special category data. Explicit consent obtained for safety purposes per Art. 9(2)(a).

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Membership administration, safe participation, and emergency contact use are each stated as specific purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data limited to relevant medical conditions rather than full medical history. Emergency contacts limited to emergency use only.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Membership data retention of 3 years post-membership stated. Retention of medical data and emergency contacts is not addressed separately.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access and rectification mentioned via club secretary. Erasure, portability, restriction, and objection rights not stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] GoCardless named as payment processor under a DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] GoCardless is a UK/EU company but its infrastructure may include non-EEA processing. Not addressed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PARTIAL] Not mentioned. Small clubs likely do not require a DPO, but this should be confirmed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not explicitly named for membership data), Art. 5 (medical and emergency contact retention periods absent), Art. 15-21 (most rights not listed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Explicitly state Art. 6(1)(b) as lawful basis for membership data processing
- Add separate retention periods for medical data and emergency contact data
- Add erasure, portability, restriction, and objection rights
- Add a breach notification commitment
- Confirm DPO assessment and state outcome
